term_limit: Senate President serves as Lt. Governor

timeline.name: Mitch Carmichael
timeline.notes: From Jackson County. Lost Republican primary in June 2020. Left office December 1, 2020.

timeline.name: William P. Cole III
timeline.notes: From Mercer County. First Republican Senate President in modern WV history, reflecting party switch of legislature.

timeline.name: Jeffrey V. Kessler
timeline.notes: From Marshall County. Initially elected as first 'Acting President' in WV Senate history (2011), then full President.

timeline.name: Earl Ray Tomblin
timeline.notes: From Logan County. Served 15 years as Senate President. Became acting governor November 15, 2010 upon Joe Manchin's resignation to US Senate. Later elected governor in his own right.

timeline.name: Keith Burdette
timeline.notes: From Wood County. Elected Senate President at age 34, youngest ever at the time.

timeline.name: Larry A. Tucker
timeline.notes: From Nicholas County. Resigned September 7, 1989 after pleading guilty to federal extortion charge. Youngest Senate President elected at the time.

timeline.name: Dan R. Tonkovich
timeline.notes: From Marshall County. Resigned to run (unsuccessfully) for governor.
